name: Week 13 Push — DELOAD (Spotlight: Cable Column Block — Lat Raise + Tri Ext + Straight-Arm Pulldown)
description: **DELOAD WEEK.** W10/W11/W12 were 3 hard weeks; W12 also added new lifts (behind-back shrugs, cool-down block). Fatigue signals are firing: the new right-shoulder symptom cluster (W12D1 — 'line from hand to shoulder,' grip-triggered shoulder pain), universal tightness, multiple soft-tissue patterns. Per the locked 3-hard/1-deload cadence, W13 is a deload. **HOLD all loads, CUT volume ~60-70%** — drop a set per exercise. Spotlights rotate to the **cable column block** (cable lat raise + incline overhead tri ext + straight-arm pulldown) — the lightest lifts of the day, perfect for a deload where the cue is the only thing being trained. **NEW: farmer carry** joins as a closing block (W12D1 shoulder-shrugs discussion outcome — loaded carry trains the same packed-shoulder pattern as the shrugs). **W12D1 takeaways baked in:** chest press 165 x 10 x 3 ran clean at the 3rd-station placement ('way easier when this is third'); incline DB went to 45 cleanly ('weirdly good no pain'); Kinesis fly 8 'stretching and relaxing everything.'

dayOverview.theme: DELOAD — first deload since W9. Motivated by W10-W12 being 3 hard weeks plus W12's new lifts, and by the W12D1 fatigue cluster: the new right-shoulder symptom pattern (line-of-sensation + grip-triggered pain), universal tightness, multiple soft-tissue patterns. HOLD every load, cut every exercise by a set. The cable column block is the spotlight precisely because it's the lightest work of the day — a deload is the right time to drill cues with nothing else competing. NEW farmer carry closing block extends the trap-chain work the shrugs started.

spotlights.why: W12D1 surfaced a real cue interaction — 'if I lead with elbow and focus on back, I feel it in the elbow even tho not using it at all.' A deload is the week to untangle that: drill lead-with-elbow WITHOUT letting the elbow take load, soft-fixed elbow, stop at shoulder height. Light load, full attention on the path.

W12D1 locked cable column 70 as the working number (first cable-column data point — W10/W11 were on Kinesis). Deload drills the setup: kneel facing away, torso-forward 'incline,' elbows pinned, full overhead stretch. Hold 70, drill the elbow pin.
Cable column ~100 working number. The lift most likely to drift into a lat-pulldown — drill the fixed slight-elbow-bend + 'pull to thighs not past' + lat squeeze. Deload load, deep cue.

dayOverview.rotationPlan: W11 chest press machine · W12 lateral raise + Kinesis cable fly · W13 cable column block — lat raise + tri ext + straight-arm pulldown (current, DELOAD) · W14 cycle back to push press + incline DB to refresh.
watchpoints: **This is a DELOAD — resist the urge to push.** Loads HOLD at W12 numbers; every exercise drops a set. If a set feels easy, that is the deload working as designed — do NOT add reps or weight. The point is to let connective tissue catch up to muscle (it lags 6-8 weeks).

**Right shoulder symptom cluster (W12D1) is the headline watchpoint.** 'Line from hand to shoulder' at 90°, raising the arm to 90° getting hard, squeezing the right hand triggering shoulder weakness/pain. The deload + cool-down + shrug/carry work is the intervention. **If the cluster is WORSE by W13D3 pull day, that flips the hypothesis from functional to structural — book a PT eval.** Don't escalate push press load until it settles.
**Chest press cut to 2 sets at 165** — W12D1 confirmed 165 x 10 x 3 ran clean at the 3rd-station slot; deload trims to 2. Hold 165, do NOT chase a 3rd set.
**Incline DB holds 45** — W12D1 went to 45 'weirdly good no pain.' Deload: 2 sets, hold 45. Drop to 40 only if the right shoulder is loud on set 1.

**NEW: farmer carry closing block** — extends the W12D1 shoulder-shrugs discussion. The packed-shoulder loaded carry trains the same down-and-back trap pattern as the behind-back shrugs. Introduce light: 2 walks, 40-50lb DBs. Cue is 'pack DOWN not up.'
Yuri band red warmup non-negotiable. Doorframe pec stretch in the morning flow add-on.

exercises.notes: Slow, big circles. Wake up scap glide before loading.
Full sequence with red band. Non-negotiable on push day — extra important this week with the right-shoulder symptom cluster active.
Ribs down, glutes tight, body in one line. Slow eccentric, 1s pause with chest 1-2" off the floor. (Deload: 10, not 12.)

sets.name: Station 1: DB Rack — Push Press + Lateral Raise (DELOAD — 2 Sets Each)

exercises.notes:
**LOAD:** 40/side (HOLD — W12D1 hit 40 x 10/10/8). **DELOAD: 2 sets, NOT 3.**
- **Feet parallel, hip-width** — locked since W7
- **Ribs DOWN before the dip** — W12D1 flagged rib flare creeping in on set 2; on a 2-set day there is no excuse for it, catch it on set 1
- **Pack DOWN, not up** — shoulder blades into the back pockets, neck long. If you have to shrug up to lock out, the load is too heavy *for the cue* — that check matters more than ever with the shoulder cluster active.
- Shallow dip ~3-4", vertical DB path, head pokes through at the top
- Stop the set the moment rib position or the packed shoulder breaks

**LOAD:** 15lb (HOLD — W12D1 hit 15 x 20 x 2, slow eccentric 'super hard'). **DELOAD: 2 sets, ~18 reps** (slightly trimmed from 20).
- **Lead with elbow, hand passive** — wrist follows, no wrist break
- **Soft elbow ~10-15°, FIXED** through the rep
- **Stop at shoulder height — never higher** (past = traps eat the rep)
- **2s eccentric** — slow descent is the stimulus
- **Scap-reset between sets** — roll shoulders, engage/disengage scapula
- Massage the right pec between sets if the shoulder talks

sets.name: Station 2: DB Rack — Incline Bench (DELOAD — 2 Sets)

exercises.notes:
**LOAD:** 45lb pair (HOLD — W12D1 hit 45 x 10 x 3, 'felt weirdly good no pain'). **DELOAD: 2 sets.** Drop to 40 only if the right shoulder is loud on set 1.
- **Bench at 30°**, NOT 45° — forearm stack is automatic at 30°
- **Shoulders packed back AND down** — pin scaps into the bench
- Elbows ~45° from torso
- 1s pause at the bottom, no DB clack at the top
- This is a deload — 45 should feel controlled, not a grind. If it grinds, the right shoulder is telling you something — drop to 40.

sets.name: Station 3: Chest Press Machine (DELOAD — 2 Sets at 165)

exercises.notes:
**LOAD:** 165 (HOLD — W12D1 ran 165 x 10 x 3 clean at this 3rd-station placement, 'way easier when this is third'). **DELOAD: 2 sets, NOT 3.** Do not chase a 3rd set just because 165 felt good last week — the deload is the cut.
- **Seat height: handles align with mid-chest / nipple line**, set BEFORE sitting
- **Shoulders pinned BACK against the back pad** — the foundation cue
- **3s eccentric** — count it
- **Press to almost extension** — leave a hair of bend
- Massage out the right pec between sets — pre-empt the referral

**LOAD:** Kinesis 8 (HOLD — W12D1 'felt great, stretching and relaxing everything'). **DELOAD: 2 sets.** This is the lift that loosens the right shoulder — protect it even on a deload.
- **Slight forward hinge at hips** — chest leads, not head
- **Soft elbow ~10-15°, FIXED throughout** — not a press, not a curl
- **"Hug a tree"** — squeeze from the chest, not the arms
- **Slow eccentric (~3s)** — let the cables pull the arms back for the deep pec stretch
- **Stretch is the stimulus** — W12D1 said the shoulder felt good at the stretch; that's the cue

sets.name: 🔆 Station 5: Cable Column Block — Lat Raise → Tri Ext → Straight-Arm Pulldown (Spotlight, DELOAD — 2 Rounds)

exercises.equipment: cable column (default — W12D1 setup) — Kinesis 3 fallback if column is occupied
exercises.notes:
**LOAD:** cable column ~30-40lb (HOLD — form-first range). **DELOAD: 2 rounds, one arm at a time.**

**🔆 SPOTLIGHT — drill the W12D1 cue interaction every rep:**
- W12D1 observation: **'if I lead with elbow and focus on back, I feel it in the elbow even tho not using it at all.'** That sensation means the elbow is becoming a load path it shouldn't be.
- **Lead with the elbow as a DIRECTION, not a lever** — the elbow points the way the arm travels; it does not bend, extend, or grip. If you feel the elbow *working*, you've turned a direction cue into a joint action. Reset: soft elbow, fixed angle, let the medial delt lift the whole arm as one unit.
- **Soft elbow ~10°, FIXED throughout** — the angle never changes from start to top
- **Stand sideways to the column**, working arm farther from the stack
- **Stop at shoulder height — higher = traps**
- **Fight the cable on the way down**, no snap-back, 2s eccentric
- **Massage the right pec between sets** — kills the crackle
- **Skip 'run line behind body' cue** — doesn't apply at this gym's geometry

**Reflection:** by round 2, is the elbow quiet (just pointing) or is it still 'feeling' the rep? If it's still feeling it, slow down and lighten — the deload is the week to get this clean.

exercises.equipment: cable column kneeling (default — W12D1 working number) — Kinesis 5 fallback if column is occupied
exercises.notes:
**LOAD:** cable column 70 (HOLD — W12D1 locked 70 as the working number; 50 was too easy). **DELOAD: 2 rounds.**

**🔆 SPOTLIGHT — drill the setup every rep:**
- **Kneel facing AWAY from the high pulley**, rope overhead
- **Torso slightly forward — that's the 'incline.'** It biases the long head; without the forward lean it's an ordinary pressdown.
- **Elbows PINNED** — only the forearms move. The elbow is the hinge and nothing else. If the elbows drift forward or flare, the long head offloads.
- **Hands shoulder-width on the rope** (long-head emphasis)
- **Full stretch at the bottom** — let the rope pull the hands behind the head
- **Stop just shy of lockout**, slow eccentric

**Reflection:** at round 2, are the elbows still pinned in the same spot they started, or have they crept forward? The pin is the lift.

exercises.equipment: cable column (default — W12D1 setup) — Kinesis 6 ≈ cable column 100 fallback
exercises.notes:
**LOAD:** cable column 100 (HOLD — W12D1 working number, done clean 'no specifics needed'). **DELOAD: 2 rounds.**

**🔆 SPOTLIGHT — drill the cues that keep this from becoming a lat pulldown:**
- **Stand a step back, hinge ~15° at the hips** — fixed hinge, the torso doesn't pump
- **Slight elbow bend (~5°), FIXED throughout** — perfectly straight arms = elbow risk; bending arms = it becomes a pulldown. The elbow holds ONE angle the whole set.
- **Pull the bar to the thighs, NOT past** — past the thighs the shoulders roll forward and the lats switch off
- **Squeeze the lats hard at the bottom** — 'oranges into the back pockets'
- **Slow controlled return to a full overhead stretch** — the stretch at the top is half the lift

**Reflection:** does it feel like a straight-arm lat movement, or has the elbow started bending to help? If the elbow is bending, that's the lat tapping out — stop the set.

sets.notes: **The spotlight block — and the lightest work of the day, which is exactly why it's the deload spotlight.** Nothing here is heavy; the entire training effect this week is the quality of the cue. One trip to the cable column covers all three (slide the attachment between exercises). 2 rounds, not 3.

sets.name: 🆕 Station 6: Farmer Carry (NEW — W12D1 shoulder-shrugs discussion outcome)

exercises.notes:
**LOAD:** 40-50lb DBs — start at 40, this is an introduction. **2 walks of ~30-45s.**

**Why this lift, now** (W12D1 shoulder-shrugs discussion — see `docs/notes/functional-bulk/shoulder-shrugs-discussion.md`):
- The behind-back shrugs gave a strong first signal W12D3 ('crackly pop, then everything aligned'). Farmer carry trains the **same packed-shoulder pattern** under a different stimulus — the traps engage tonically to hold the shoulder packed while you walk.
- It directly drills the **'pack DOWN, not up'** correction the user flagged W12D1.

**Cues (drill every step):**
- **Stand TALL** — ribs down, crown of the head reaching up
- **Shoulders pulled DOWN and BACK** — into the back pockets, NEVER shrugged toward the ears. This is the whole point of the lift. The DBs will try to pull the shoulders up; resist that the entire walk.
- **Neck long, eyes forward** — no chin poke
- **Walk a straight line at a deliberate pace**, breathing steadily — don't rush
- **Grip firm but not death-clenched** — note whether a hard right-hand squeeze provokes the shoulder (W12D1 grip→shoulder coupling watch)

**What to watch (REPORT BACK):** does keeping the shoulder packed under the carry feel like it RELIEVES the right-shoulder cluster (like the shrugs did), or aggravate it? Same hypothesis test as the shrugs — relief = functional pattern, aggravation = structural, escalate to PT.

**DELOAD note:** keep this genuinely light — 2 walks, 40lb. It's a new lift in a deload week; build the cue, the load comes later.

sets.notes: Quick closing block at the DB rack on the way to the cool-down. 2 walks, ~30-45s each, light load. Focus is the packed-shoulder posture, not the weight. Total ~3 min including rest.

sets.name: Cool-Down — 5 Min Fascia/Recovery Block (Permanent Every-Gym-Day Closing Block)

exercises.notes: 30s each arm at MID elbow position. Arm bent 90°, forearm on the door frame, step through + rotate the torso AWAY. Pec minor lengthen — the antagonist to the upper-trap chain that's been loaded all session.
Passive hang from the pull-up bar. **Don't engage** — let the shoulders unpack and the spine decompress. Especially valuable this week for the right-shoulder cluster.
30s each side on the mat. Lie back, draw one knee across the body, opposite arm extended. Breathe into the rib cage on the rotated side.
45s each side. Front leg + back leg at 90°. Sink hips toward the floor — hip-rotator decompression.
60s slow breathing. Knees wide, big toes touching, arms extended forward. Breathe into the back ribs — parasympathetic shift before leaving the gym.

sets.notes: **5 minutes of recovery work, non-negotiable closing block (permanent since W12D3).** On a deload week this block matters even more — it's the connective-tissue-catch-up window. Goes after the last lift, before walking out.

tips: **DELOAD WEEK — hold every load, cut every exercise a set.** First deload since W9. If something feels easy, that's the design. Do not add reps or weight.

**Spotlight: the cable column block** (cable lat raise + incline overhead tri ext + straight-arm pulldown) — the lightest work of the day, drilled deep because a deload has nothing else competing for attention.
**Cable lat raise: 'lead with elbow' is a DIRECTION, not a joint action.** If you feel the elbow working, you've corrupted the cue (W12D1 observation). Reset to a fixed soft elbow.
**Right shoulder symptom cluster is the headline watchpoint.** If it's worse by W13D3 pull day, the hypothesis flips to structural — book a PT eval.
**Chest press 2 sets at 165, incline DB 2 sets at 45** — both held from W12D1's clean numbers. Deload trims the volume, not the load.
**Pack DOWN, not up** — push press, incline DB, chest press, lateral raise, farmer carry all carry the cue. If you must shrug up to finish a rep, the load is too heavy for the cue — drop it.

**NEW: farmer carry closing block** — 2 walks, 40-50lb DBs, 'tall posture, packed shoulders, breathe.' Extends the trap-chain work the shrugs started. Report whether it relieves or aggravates the right shoulder.
**Cool-down stays — 5-min block, permanent every gym day.** On a deload it's the connective-tissue catch-up window.
Yuri band red warmup non-negotiable. Doorframe pec stretch in the morning flow add-on.
Rotation plan: W14 cycles back to push press + incline DB to refresh those cues.
Rest 2 min DB rack work, 2.5 min chest press, 75s cables + carry.
formPhilosophy: Deload week — the first since W9, locked in by the 3-hard/1-deload cadence. The training effect this week is NOT load; it's letting connective tissue catch up to muscle (it lags 6-8 weeks) and drilling cues with the volume turned down. The cable column spotlight, the cool-down, and the new farmer carry all serve recovery and pattern quality. Building decades, not weeks.
